title: oxalis pedunculata
intro: Oxalis pedunculata, also known as the red wood sorrel, is a perennial plant native to the Andes mountains in South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a natural diuretic and to help alleviate symptoms of kidney stones and urinary tract infections. The health benefits of Oxalis pedunculata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support the immune system. Oxalis pedunculata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
